channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP senator moves to block feds from disposing of border wall materials amid auction backlash
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty, R-Tenn. is introducing legislation to stop the federal government from auctioning border wall materials at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress nears funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders are nearing the unveiling of a spending agreement that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton, and other natura
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Republicans in key red state launch campaign to elect ‘true’ conservatives ahead of Trump return
item.description: Conservatives in the most populous red state in America are launching a new Texas Republican Leadership Fund to elect “true" conservatives to crucial leadership roles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden’s Cabinet officials stand by statements of support as term draws to a close
item.description: With a month left in office, Biden’s Cabinet reaffirms support, stating confidence in his leadership and fitness despite ongoing controversy and debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas House panel subpoenas death row inmate Robert Roberson a second time
item.description: The new subpoena comes after lawmakers say Ken Paxton’s office stalled a previous effort to get Roberson’s legislative testimony about his conviction in “shaken baby” case.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden administration pressuring African country to adopt lax abortion laws in exchange for foreign aid: report
item.description: President Biden's administration is facing further accusations of using foreign aid to push African countries to adopt more permissive abortion laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archivist says Equal Rights Amendment can't be certified as Democrats push Biden to recognize it
item.description: The archivist and deputy archivist of the United States have issued a rare joint statement to say that the 1970s-era Equal Rights Amendment cannot be certified without further action by Congress or the courts.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: America First Works says its voter-targeting made a difference as Trump picks several leaders for new admin
item.description: America First Works and its partner AFPI seek to forward Trump world principles and policies, and reach out to voters in strategic ways with unique programs like Project 19.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Freedom Caucus reelects Andy Harris as chair
item.description: Rep. Andy Harris (R-Md.) will chair the House Freedom Caucus through the end of 2025, members of the group decided Monday night, cementing his leadership of the hard-line conservative caucus.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction because of Supreme Court immunity ruling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court won’t block order directing Peter Navarro to turn over emails
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to weigh Peter Navarro’s bid to reverse a court order directing him to turn over hundreds of emails sent during his tenure as an adviser in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: Trump plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: ABC News to pay $15M in Trump defamation lawsuit
item.description: Court documents publicly filed on Saturday and obtained by NewsNation detailed the settlement's conditions.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU: Officials should enact law 'firewall' ahead of Trump 2nd term
item.description: ACLU leaders told press Wednesday that state and city governments should prepare for the implementation of ideas from Project 2025.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
